properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.enable
properties.description: Master switch for WebSocket cluster mode. Default false — single-node mode with zero cluster overhead. Only when true does the cluster auto-configuration activate.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.node-id
properties.description: Unique identifier for this node. Null or empty auto-generates a UUID.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.redis.cluster-nodes
properties.description: Comma-separated Redis Cluster seed nodes (host:port,host:port,...). Empty (default) = standalone/sentinel via redis.uri. Non-empty selects the RedisClusterClient transport path — the broker, registry, heartbeat and reaper all run against Redis Cluster (since 1.9.0-RC7). Mutually exclusive with reliable.enable in RC7 (Redis Streams XADD/XREADGROUP cross-slot semantics are deferred); use the standalone/sentinel path for at-least-once reliable broadcast.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.redis.uri
properties.description: Redis connection URI. Topology is selected by the scheme: redis://host:port (standalone), redis-sentinel://host:port/?sentinelMasterId=... (sentinel). Use rediss:// for TLS and redis://:password@host for auth — Redis is a trusted control plane and MUST be secured in production.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.heartbeat-interval-seconds
properties.description: How often this node writes its heartbeat to Redis.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.heartbeat-timeout-seconds
properties.description: How long before a missing heartbeat marks a node as dead.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reconciliation-interval-seconds
properties.description: Slow-path reconciliation sweep interval — backstop for missed keyspace notifications (fire-and-forget, no replay).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.drain-timeout-seconds
properties.description: Graceful-shutdown drain window (seconds). On shutdown the node enters DRAINING and waits up to this long for in-flight cross-node deliveries to settle before deregistering. Default 0 = no wait (instant deregister, pre-1.9.0 behavior). Set a positive value to opt into a bounded grace window (a fixed wait, not a session-count drain).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reconnect-jitter-max-seconds
properties.description: Max jitter applied before a DEGRADED to RESYNC re-registration, to avoid reconnect storms when many nodes recover simultaneously.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.redis-loss-grace-period-ms
properties.description: Grace period (ms) before a transport loss degrades the node state machine (debounce). A blip shorter than this will not flap the node or trigger on-redis-loss=close-all. The broker's own state still flips immediately for truthful health. 0 = instant degrade (1.8.0 behavior).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.on-redis-loss
properties.description: Behavior when the cluster transport (Redis) is lost. DEGRADE_TO_LOCAL (default) keeps local sessions alive and continues local fan-out, pausing only cross-node traffic. CLOSE_ALL closes all local sessions (fail-fast / load-shed).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.on-publish-failure
properties.description: Behavior when a cluster publish/unicast fails or a message is too large. LOG (default) logs a warning; DROP silently drops (only the publishFailures counter increments).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.registry-read-cache-ttl-ms
properties.description: Local sessionId-to-nodeId cache TTL (ms) for the unicast hot path.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.registry-read-cache-max-size
properties.description: Max entries in the local sessionId-to-nodeId read cache (unicast hot path). The TTL governs only reuse, not eviction, so without a hard cap a node unicasting to many distinct LIVE remote sessions would grow this map without bound. Bounded LRU eviction caps the footprint. 0 or less = unbounded (legacy behavior, not recommended).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.command-timeout-ms
properties.description: Redis command timeout (ms) for the cluster control plane. Bounds how long any single Redis operation (including the unicast hot-path registry lookup) can block when Redis is unreachable — much lower than Lettuce's 60s default.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.pubsub-connections
properties.description: Number of Redis Pub/Sub SUBSCRIBE connections to spread inbound decode across by channel hash. 1 (default) = single connection, unchanged. Use 2-4 only when a node approaches the single Lettuce pub/sub connection decode ceiling (~80k msg/s). Range [1,16]. Redis-Pub/Sub-specific.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.nats.servers
properties.description: Comma-separated NATS server URLs (nats://host:port,...). When non-empty, the NatsClusterBroker replaces the Redis Pub/Sub broker (transport only; SessionRegistry and heartbeat stay on Redis). Requires io.nats:jnats on the classpath. Empty (default) = Redis broker.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.nats.registry
properties.description: When true (and nats.servers is set), run the SessionRegistry/heartbeat/reaper on NATS JetStream KV instead of Redis - a fully NATS-only deployment (no Redis). Requires a JetStream-enabled NATS server (nats-server -js). Default false (mixed: NATS broker + Redis registry).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.message-max-size-bytes
properties.description: Max serialized cluster message size in bytes. Larger messages are not published to the cluster (local delivery is unaffected); handled per on-publish-failure. 0 means unlimited.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.session-registry-write-rate
properties.description: Max sustained session register+deregister writes per second per node. Absorbs reconnect storms by coalescing and throttling registry writes (never dropping a register). Under this rate, writes pass straight through with no added latency. 0 means unlimited (pure pass-through).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.enable
properties.description: Opt-in at-least-once reliable broadcast (reliableBroadcast()). Disabled by default — when false there are no consumer threads, no extra transport connections, and reliableBroadcast() throws. Existing topicMessage() (Pub/Sub, at-most-once) is unaffected either way. Transport-agnostic: Redis Streams in all-Redis / mixed deployments; NATS JetStream when nats.registry=true (since V1.9.0-RC13).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.stream-max-len
properties.description: Per-URI Redis Stream MAXLEN (approximate) for reliable broadcast — the at-least-once retention window. A node offline longer than this many entries of history on a URI misses the trimmed ones (bounded gap). (NATS: JetStream stream max_msgs.)

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.poll-block-ms
properties.description: XREADGROUP BLOCK timeout (ms) for the reliable consume loop. (NATS: JetStream fetch block timeout.)

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.poll-count
properties.description: XREADGROUP COUNT (max entries per read) for the reliable consume loop. (NATS: JetStream fetch batch size.)

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.dedup-window
properties.description: Per-URI ring size of recently-acked stream entry ids, to de-duplicate in-process redelivery (at-least-once still allows cross-crash duplicates; handlers should be idempotent). (NATS: in-process dedup ring on JetStream stream sequence numbers.)

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.group-destroy-idle-ms
properties.description: Idle window (ms) a dead node's reliable consumer group must be inactive before reconciliation may destroy it on heartbeat-expiry. A node id is stable, so a crashed node that restarts reuses its group; destroying it too eagerly would wipe the retained offset/PEL and the restarted node would skip its backlog (data loss). This window keeps a recently-active group intact past any realistic crash-restart gap. 0 or less = never destroy on expiry (pure retain; rely on stream MAXLEN trimming). (NATS: idle-gate on ConsumerInfo.delivered.lastActive; same retain-on-doubt semantics.)

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.enable
properties.description: Opt-in HMAC authentication of cross-node envelopes (anti-forgery). Disabled by default. When true, every envelope is signed (HMAC-SHA256) and inbound envelopes with a missing/invalid tag are rejected — closing the threat where a Redis writer forges originNodeId / injects / force-closes sessions. Enable cluster-wide (shared secret); see the rolling-upgrade note in the release notes.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.secret
properties.description: Shared HMAC secret (UTF-8). REQUIRED when auth.enable=true. Externalize via an environment variable or secret manager (e.g. ${CLUSTER_AUTH_SECRET}); never hardcode. Use >= 32 chars. Redacted in logs.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.permissive
properties.description: Migration mode: when true, UNSIGNED inbound envelopes are accepted (while still signing outbound) so HMAC can be rolled out node-by-node without cross-node message loss; flip to false (strict) once all nodes sign. Default false.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.trace-propagation.enable
properties.description: Opt-in W3C TraceContext propagation across cluster hops (since 1.9.0-RC6). Disabled by default — when false, behavior and wire bytes are identical to pre-RC6 (no MDC mutation, no envelope traceparent field). When true, the outbound publisher injects the current traceparent (from MDC / OpenTelemetry context) into the envelope, and inbound consumers extract it onto MDC for the duration of dispatch so logs and downstream spans correlate across nodes. Opt-in to keep zero-dep deployments byte-identical.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.room.enable
properties.description: Opt-in room-scoped routing with per-room node-targeted delivery (since 1.10.0-RC1). Disabled by default — when false there are no room beans, no room subscriptions, and no ROOM_BROADCAST envelopes are produced (behavior identical to 1.9.0). When true, a ClusterRoomRegistry bean is wired and roomMessage(uri, room, msg) reaches only the nodes hosting members of that room (fan-out N/k, k = nodes with members) by reusing the existing per-node unicast channel — a real reduction for bounded rooms in large clusters even under random load-balanced placement. A hot room whose members span every node sees no reduction (and costs k targeted sends vs 1 global publish); watch netty.cluster.room.fanout.target_nodes to see whether you are getting reduction.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.room.node-set-cache-ttl-ms
properties.description: Local cache TTL (ms) for the per-room node-set on the room send hot path (nodesForRoom), mirroring registry-read-cache-ttl-ms. A stale node-set self-heals within this window (at worst targeting a node that no longer hosts the room, or briefly missing a node that just gained a member). The cache is invalidated wholesale on NODE_LEFT.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.enable
properties.description: Opt-in user-addressed delivery + per-user offline queue (since 1.10.0-RC2). Disabled by default — when false there are no offline beans, the UserIdResolver is never created, and the cluster session hook passes emptyMap to register exactly as RC1 (byte-identical: no userId resolution, no presence binding, no offline queue). When true, a UserIdResolver (default HandshakeUserIdResolver), UserRegistry (default RedisUserRegistry) and OfflineQueueStore (default RedisOfflineQueueStore) are wired, and sendToUser(userId, msg) delivers to an online user or enqueues for FIFO backfill on reconnect. At-least-once to offline users within the retention window; handlers must be idempotent. SECURITY: the default resolver trusts a handshake value VERBATIM and is convenience/testing only — production MUST supply a UserIdResolver bean that derives userId from the AUTHENTICATED principal.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.user-id-source
properties.description: Where the default HandshakeUserIdResolver reads the userId: query:<name> or header:<name> (e.g. query:userId, header:X-User-Id). Malformed/blank falls back to query:userId. CONVENIENCE/TESTING ONLY — the value is trusted verbatim with NO authentication; production MUST supply its own UserIdResolver bean deriving userId from the authenticated principal.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.max-messages-per-user
properties.description: Per-user Redis Stream MAXLEN (approximate) for the offline queue — the at-least-once retention bound. Older messages are trimmed past this (a bounded gap, metered netty.cluster.offline.dropped_retention).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.ttl-seconds
properties.description: Per-message age cap (seconds) for an offline-queued message. Lazily dropped on drain (an entry older than this is not delivered) and bounded by the stream MAXLEN trim. Default 604800 (7 days).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.drain-batch-size
properties.description: Max messages drained + delivered per connect (the rest drain on a subsequent connect).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.offline.drain-lock-ms
properties.description: Per-userId drain lock TTL (ms; SET NX PX). The lock makes drain EXCLUSIVE per userId so two concurrent multi-device reconnects do not double-deliver (the non-holder's drain returns empty). It auto-expires so a crashed drainer cannot wedge the user's queue.

values.description: Keep local sessions alive, continue local fan-out, pause cross-node traffic (default).
Close all local sessions when the transport is lost.
Log a warning on publish failure (default).
